content: Diagnosis confirmed by hyperglycemia criteria (same as T2DM: HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL, random ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms) PLUS evidence of autoimmune beta-cell destruction. Diagnostic workup: measure diabetes autoantibodies (GAD65 most sensitive, IA-2, ZnT8, IAA); low/undetectable C-peptide indicates insulin deficiency. DKA presentation common at diagnosis: glucose >250 mg/dL, pH <7.3, bicarbonate <18 mEq/L, positive ketones. Differentiation from T2DM critical: younger age, lean body habitus, rapid onset, ketosis-prone favor T1DM. Staging system proposed: Stage 1 (normoglycemia + ≥2 autoantibodies), Stage 2 (dysglycemia + autoantibodies), Stage 3 (clinical diabetes). Screening autoantibodies in first-degree relatives considered for prevention trials.
